$comment: Neutral shipped <sys/mman.h> (POSIX) — c14 (linux) + c15c (macOS). The memory-mapped-I/O surface SQLite's os_unix.c uses for its mmap'd page cache: mmap/munmap + Linux mremap (resize, under SQLITE_MMAP_READWRITE). PROT_READ/PROT_WRITE (page protections) + MAP_SHARED all AGREE between Linux and macOS (1/2/1 — verified against xnu/bsd/sys/mman.h), so they stay flat. mremap + MREMAP_MAYMOVE are LINUX-ONLY: macOS has no mremap(). MREMAP_MAYMOVE carries an elf-only `variants` entry so it is NOT injected on macho (a macho reference fails loud — truthful). The mremap SYMBOL stays declared on both formats but is an UNREFERENCED extern on macho (the errno __error/__errno_location pattern — only the format-referenced symbol links; SQLite guards mremap behind HAVE_MREMAP, Linux-only, so it is never imported on macOS). MAP_FAILED is the `((void*)-1)` sentinel (a pointer → the MACROS surface). off_t is i64 (LP64, both). Gated availableObjectFormats:[elf,macho]: elf=the WSL run target, macho=the macos-latest CI leg (Apple Silicon).

symbols.name: mmap
symbols.signature: fn(ptr<void>, u64, i32, i32, i32, i64) -> ptr<void>
